title: What is MATLAB?
body: MATLAB computes whatever you type, instantly. A '%' starts a comment it ignores.
code:
% This is a comment
5 + 3        % MATLAB shows: ans = 8

title: Variables & Assignment
body: '=' stores the right-hand value into a name you can reuse. End a line with ';' to hide its output.
code:
x = 5;        % store 5 in x
y = x * 2;    % y is now 10
y             % shows: y = 10

title: Vectors & Arrays
body: A vector is a list of numbers written in square brackets. Indexing starts at 1, not 0.

title: Doing Math
body: Use + - * / for math. Add a dot (.* ./ .^) to apply it to each element of a vector.

title: for Loops
body: 'for' repeats code over a range like '1:5' (1 through 5). Every 'for' needs a matching 'end'.
code:
for i = 1:5
    disp(i)   % shows 1, then 2, ... up to 5
end

title: Milestone: Plotting a Graph
body: Make an x vector, compute y from it, and 'plot(x, y)' draws the curve — numbers into a picture.

title: The Shape of a C Program
body: C starts at 'main'. Code lives inside { } and every statement ends with a semicolon.

title: Variables & Types
body: Declare the type, then the name: 'int' (whole numbers), 'double' (decimals), 'char' (one letter).

title: Printing with printf
body: 'printf' prints text; '\n' is a new line; '%d' is a placeholder for an int passed after the text.

title: Making Decisions: if / else
body: 'if' runs when a condition is true, 'else' when false. '==' tests equality; a single '=' assigns.

title: for Loops
body: A 'for' has three parts: start, condition, update. 'i++' adds 1 each pass through the loop.

title: Milestone: A Tiny Full Program
body: Include, main, a loop, and printf together make a complete, runnable C program.

title: C++ vs C
body: C++ extends C: same basics, plus easy I/O (cout/cin) and classes. Print with '#include <iostream>'.

title: cout and cin
body: 'cout <<' prints (chain with '<<'), 'endl' ends the line, 'cin >>' reads input into a variable.

title: Variables & Types
body: Same types as C, plus 'string' for text and 'bool' for true/false.

title: Functions
body: A function names reusable code: return type, name, inputs. 'return' sends a value back.

title: if / else and Loops
body: Decisions and loops work just like C — a loop repeats while an if checks a value each pass.

title: Milestone: Your First Struct
body: A struct bundles related data into one type; read its fields with a dot (p.name).
